layman_headline: Fear rules the market — no clean setup worth risking capital on right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance at 56.5%, meaning money is flowing away from altcoins and into Bitcoin for safety. Every altcoin candidate we looked at has significant problems: some have crashed too hard with no bottom signal, others have already run up too much. Ethereum is the least bad option with its RSI approaching oversold territory, but its chart structure is still bearish and it hasn't found solid support yet. The smart move is to wait for a cleaner entry rather than force a trade in a weak market.
layman_action: Stay patient and keep your cash ready. Wait for Ethereum to pull back to the $1,800 support level before considering an entry. Don't chase anything in this fearful market — better setups will come.
expert_reasoning: None of the swing candidates offer a compelling risk/reward right now. BONK is in capitulation with a -2.0 chart bias and Three Black Crows — catching this falling knife is reckless despite the oversold RSI. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but RSI at 59, +17% in 7 days, and a poor 0.38x risk/reward to nearest resistance make chasing it unwise. DEXE is RSI 71 overbought after a 63% 5-day surge. ETH is the watchlist leader: RSI at 39 (approaching oversold — our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), $1,800 support is clearly defined, and the Blockworks note about ETH derivatives resetting suggests positioning is cleaning up. However, the bearish chart structure (below all MAs, -0.70 bias) means we need price to prove $1,800 support before committing. I'm setting this as a conditional buy at $1,800 with a stop at $1,740.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to crush altcoin performance — ETH could bleed further
Weekend trading with geopolitical risk (oil supply concerns per CryptoSlate) could trigger broad selloff
ETH is below all major moving averages — trend is definitively bearish until proven otherwise
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a decisive bounce here with volume would trigger a BUY
BTC weekend price stability — any drop below $63,000 would likely drag all alts down further
ZEC momentum (+9.1% 7d) following Zcash 50K TPS node news — could become a swing candidate if it pulls back
market_context: Market is in Fear (28/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC itself is relatively stable (+1.4% 7d) but geopolitical weekend risk around oil supply adds uncertainty.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with high BTC dominance — patience is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed the 30 threshold needed for a high-conviction contrarian entry.
Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) has been flat around 25-28 all week, suggesting no capitulation flush yet that would mark a clean bottom for aggressive buying.
Bitcoin trading through a dangerous weekend with 20% of world oil supply at risk — adds macro uncertainty that argues for caution over aggressive entries.
ETH derivatives reset noted, suggesting overleveraged positions are being cleared — this is a precondition for a healthy bounce but not yet a buy trigger.
No volume breakout signals detected across any candidate — confirming that no high-conviction setups exist this cycle.
